_risk_level_doc: Mechanical rename of a script and its string references; no logic changes; no infrastructure-critical files touched.

requirement.original: Rename `spec-continue` → `spec-update` throughout the codebase (the script, its references in INDEX files and commit.md, and the test harness). Additionally, cycle 20260525-050824 had CLOSE: NO due to hardcoded flock timeouts — confirm whether those timeouts are already parameterized in the current code, and if so, identify whether any genuine hardcoded values remain to be fixed.
requirement.clarified: Rename scripts/step7-spec-continue.py to scripts/step7-spec-update.py and update all live-code references including the STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED trace marker to STEP7_SPEC_UPDATE_DISPATCHED. Confirm that score-inject.sh and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh already use LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" (fix landed); identify score-update.sh:77 as a pre-existing false-positive exempt under Standard 2 baseline provision.
requirement.what: Script rename + reference update + trace marker rename; flock timeout status confirmation (already fixed, no dev action needed)
requirement.why: The /spec-continue command was already renamed to /spec-update in a prior cycle but the underlying Step 7 harness script and all its references were not updated. The 050824 CLOSE: NO blocking items have been addressed but the rename was not.

root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The command rename (spec-continue → spec-update) was completed independently of the script rename
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Naming inconsistency; test harness path and marker assertions would fail if anyone runs tests against a repo where only the command was renamed
